item.title:
IRAN THREATENS OIL BLOCKADE IN STRAIT OF HORMUZ
“We will not allow even a single liter of oil to pass through the Strait of Hormuz for the benefit of the US, the Zionist regime or their partners.”
item.description:
IRAN THREATENS OIL BLOCKADE IN STRAIT OF HORMUZ

“We will not allow even a single liter of oil to pass through the Strait of Hormuz for the benefit of the US, the Zionist regime or their partners.”

item.title:
GET CAUGHT UP
🔸International Energy Agency may propose releasing 300–400 million barrels from reserves.
🔸Brent crude still climbed above $90 despite the potential release.
🔸G7 ministers will meet later to discuss a coordinated stockpile release.
🔸Markets turned volatile after mixed signals from U.S. officials.
🔸Military strikes continue across the Middle East.
🔸Three ships were hit, and drones fell near Dubai Airport.
item.description:
GET CAUGHT UP

🔸International Energy Agency may propose releasing 300–400 million barrels from reserves.

🔸Brent crude still climbed above $90 despite the potential release.

🔸G7 ministers will meet later to discuss a coordinated stockpile release.

🔸Markets turned volatile after mixed signals from U.S. officials.

🔸Military strikes continue across the Middle East.

🔸Three ships were hit, and drones fell near Dubai Airport.

item.title: 🚨 IRAN’S REVOLUTIONARY GUARDS SAY THEY FIRED AT THAI-FLAGGED MAYUREE NAREE BULK VESSEL IN THE STARIT OF HORMUZ - TASNIM
item.description: 🚨 IRAN’S REVOLUTIONARY GUARDS SAY THEY FIRED AT THAI-FLAGGED MAYUREE NAREE BULK VESSEL IN THE STARIT OF HORMUZ - TASNIM

item.title: BINANCE - FILES LAWSUIT AGAINST WALL STREET JOURNAL OVER REPORTING
item.description: BINANCE - FILES LAWSUIT AGAINST WALL STREET JOURNAL OVER REPORTING

item.title:
🚨 IRAN SAYS OIL WILL REACH $200 A BARREL, WARNS OF 'CONTINUOUS STRIKES'
Iran will switch from "reciprocal hits" after attacks to continuous strikes on adversaries, and the U.S. will not be able to control oil prices, the spokesperson for Tehran's Khatam al-Anbiya military command headquarters said on Wednesday.
"We won't allow even one litre of oil to reach the U.S., Zionists (Israel) and their partners. Any vessel or tanker bound to them will be a legitimate target," Ebrahim Zolfaqari said.
"Get ready for the oil barrel to be at $200 because the oil price depends on the regional security which you have destabilised," he added
item.description:
🚨 IRAN SAYS OIL WILL REACH $200 A BARREL, WARNS OF 'CONTINUOUS STRIKES'

Iran will switch from "reciprocal hits" after attacks to continuous strikes on adversaries, and the U.S. will not be able to control oil prices, the spokesperson for Tehran's Khatam al-Anbiya military command headquarters said on Wednesday.

"We won't allow even one litre of oil to reach the U.S., Zionists (Israel) and their partners. Any vessel or tanker bound to them will be a legitimate target," Ebrahim Zolfaqari said.

"Get ready for the oil barrel to be at $200 because the oil price depends on the regional security which you have destabilised," he added

item.description: The BBC is told students have been placed under lockdown conditions in the school buildings.

item.title: Pop music's bias towards English is fading, says Spotify
item.description: Songs in 16 languages featured in the global chart last year, as genres like Brazilian Funk explode.

item.title: Mortgage rates rise and deals pulled over Iran war turmoil
item.description: Average mortgage rates hit highest since last August in the biggest upheaval since the mini-Budget.

item.title: IEA preparing record release of oil reserves
item.description: Plan underlines fears for the global economy from energy shock triggered by the Middle East war

item.title: Inside the Russian explosives plot that targeted the UK
item.description: Aleksandr Suranovas, charged with carrying out an act of terrorism for Russia, speaks to the BBC.

item.title: Gulf disruption chokes sulphur flows supporting swaths of global industry
item.description: Shortage spurs surge in prices and threatens sectors from fertiliser to microchips as impact of war in Iran widens

item.title: The economic consequences of war with Iran
item.description: There are lasting lessons about energy resilience to be learnt from this conflict

item.title: Iranians rethink the price of regime change
item.description: Destructive US and Israeli war and Islamic republic’s resilience have alarmed even those who supported foreign intervention

item.title: Lasers, radars and drones: Middle East war spurs hunt for cheaper air defence
item.description: Complex systems built to counter enemy aircraft and large missiles are not cost effective for modern warfare

item.title: Government approves Met request to ban Al Quds Day march
item.description: The government grants a request from the Metropolitan Police to ban a march due to take place in London on Sunday.

item.title: Trump administration’s mixed messaging sparks wild swings in oil markets
item.description: Confusion over US naval escorts in Strait of Hormuz exposes lack of clarity over war aims

item.title: Gale-force winds to batter UK as snow and cold set for comeback
item.description: Strong winds are likely across many parts of the UK over the next few days before turning colder with some wintry showers at the end of the week, as Simon King explains.

item.title: Iran war cost will be passed to consumers, shipping giant boss tells BBC
item.description: The head of the world's second biggest shipping firm called for freedom of navigation to be restored.

item.title: Jeffrey Epstein had two key aides - why do they still control his money and secrets?
item.description: Richard Kahn and Darren Indyke administer Epstein’s estate - court filings allege complicity in his crimes.

item.title: The Aldi-style disruptors who could be about to shake up the vets market
item.description: As pet owners complain of rising prices, independent practices want to take on the big chains.

item.title: University tuition fees system is a 'mess', says Clegg
item.description: The ex-deputy PM told the BBC he would "take on the chin" any criticism surrounding his involvement

item.title: Oil swings as traders parse mixed messages on Strait of Hormuz
item.description: White House says US energy secretary’s claim that navy escorted tanker through key waterway was wrong

item.title: First Mandelson files expected to be published on Wednesday
item.description: The documents are expected to detail parts of the process prior to Lord Mandelson's appointment as ambassador.

item.title: Hereditary peers to be removed from Lords as bill passes
item.description: The bill abolishes the 92 seats reserved for peers who inherit their titles through their families.

item.title: *TRUMP: IF IRAN HAS PUT MINES IN THE HORMUZ WE WANT THEM REMOVED
item.description: *TRUMP: IF IRAN HAS PUT MINES IN THE HORMUZ WE WANT THEM REMOVED

item.title:
TRUMP WARNS IRAN ON HORMUZ MINES
“If Iran has placed any mines in the Hormuz Strait—and we have no reports that they have—they must be removed immediately! Failure to do so will bring military consequences the likes of which have never been seen. If they remove them, it will be a giant step in the right direction!” — President Donald J. Trump
item.description:
TRUMP WARNS IRAN ON HORMUZ MINES

“If Iran has placed any mines in the Hormuz Strait—and we have no reports that they have—they must be removed immediately! Failure to do so will bring military consequences the likes of which have never been seen. If they remove them, it will be a giant step in the right direction!”
— President Donald J. Trump

item.title: Jury restriction plan clears first hurdle despite Labour dissent
item.description: The justice secretary says the limits are required to stem a growing backlog of cases in crown courts.
